skill.name: office-productivity
skill.description: Office productivity workflow for coordinating document creation, spreadsheet automation, presentation generation, format conversion, and office application integrations.
skill.summary: Coordinate office document, spreadsheet, presentation, and conversion workflows across common productivity tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown backticks, skill names, fenced examples, and ordinary descriptive text. No executable code, network access, cryptography, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Skill References Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backtick-wrapped skill names and fenced copy-paste prompts as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Manual review shows these are Markdown documentation examples, not executable code. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: SKILL.md contains only Markdown text and no Ruby, shell scripts, or command execution operators outside documentation formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is Markdown formatting for skill names and prompts. There is no executable source file or interpreter context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but manual review found no cryptographic functions, hashes, ciphers, keys, or password handling. The matches appear to come from ordinary words and office workflow text. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The referenced lines describe templates, design steps, formats, and skill names rather than security-sensitive algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No line contains cryptographic code or security configuration. The suspicious terms are part of normal office productivity prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a network reconnaissance pattern at a presentation skill reference. Manual review found no network host discovery, socket use, external URL, or command invocation. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The line only lists libreoffice-impress as a related presentation skill, which has no reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a plain Markdown bullet naming a presentation tool. There is no network API, shell command, or target address.

content.value_statement: Office teams often need consistent documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and converted files across different tools. This skill organizes those tasks into clear workflow phases for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides document creation workflows for LibreOffice Writer, Word documents, and PDF handling.
Organizes spreadsheet automation tasks for LibreOffice Calc, Excel reports, and Google Sheets.
Maps presentation generation steps for LibreOffice Impress, PowerPoint, and HTML slide workflows.
Defines format conversion phases for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDF outputs.
Includes quality gates for formatting, formulas, presentations, conversions, automation, and file organization.
Lists related workflow bundles for development, documentation, and database tasks.
limitations: It is a workflow guide and does not include executable automation scripts.
It depends on separate office, spreadsheet, presentation, and storage skills being available.
It does not validate generated documents or converted files by itself.
It does not provide direct authentication or API setup for Google Workspace or Microsoft Office.

use_cases.title: Create Standard Reports
use_cases.description: Plan a repeatable workflow for reports that combine document text, spreadsheet data, charts, and final PDF exports.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Presentations
use_cases.description: Coordinate slide generation, chart creation, graphics, and format exports across PowerPoint and LibreOffice tools.

use_cases.title: Automate Office File Handling
use_cases.description: Structure mail merge, file organization, document conversion, and quality checks for recurring office tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Document Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the office-productivity workflow to plan a formatted business document. Include the document structure, required inputs, target format, and quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Spreadsheet Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the office-productivity workflow to create a spreadsheet report plan. Include data sources, formulas, charts, export formats, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Presentation Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the office-productivity workflow to coordinate a presentation package from source data. Include slide sections, charts, graphics, speaker materials, and export targets.

prompt_templates.title: Design an End-to-End Office Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the office-productivity workflow to design an end-to-end pipeline for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, conversion, file organization, and final quality gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a monthly sales report with charts and PDF export.
output: Document phase: define report sections, headings, brand formatting, and export target.
Spreadsheet phase: import sales data, calculate totals, create charts, and verify formulas.
Quality phase: check formatting, formulas, chart labels, PDF output, and file organization.

output_examples.input: Create a training presentation from spreadsheet data.
output: Presentation phase: define slide outline, data-driven visuals, notes, and target slide format.
Graphics phase: create charts or diagrams, then integrate them into the deck.
Review phase: confirm completeness, visual consistency, export quality, and source file storage.

output_examples.input: Convert office files and organize the outputs.
output: Conversion phase: identify source formats, choose target formats, and process files in batches.
Verification phase: inspect converted files for layout, formula, and content issues.
Organization phase: place outputs in the required folder structure with clear names.

best_practices: Define source data, target formats, and quality gates before generating files.
Use the most specific related skill for each document, spreadsheet, presentation, or conversion task.
Review generated outputs manually when formatting, formulas, or client-facing material matter.
anti_patterns: Do not use the workflow as a replacement for dedicated office application setup.
Do not skip quality checks after format conversion or batch generation.
Do not assume unavailable related skills can be invoked without installation or configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill create office files by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide that coordinates related skills for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and conversions.

faq.question: Which office formats does it cover?
faq.answer: It references ODT, ODS, ODP, ODG, ODB, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, PDF, and Google Workspace formats.

faq.question: Can it help with spreadsheet automation?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines spreadsheet structure, formulas, data import, chart generation, report export, and verification steps.

faq.question: Can it coordinate presentation generation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers slide templates, data-driven slides, charts, graphics, animations, and export workflows.

faq.question: Does it require external applications?
faq.answer: Yes. Practical use depends on separate LibreOffice, Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, or related automation skills.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown workflow instructions and no executable code, network calls, or prompt injection.
